listitem.para: Source image. The input can be a grayscale image or an RGB image.
Output image with globally enhanced contrast.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: This function applies global histogram equalization to improve image contrast. The source image is converted to uint8 before the histogram is computed. The cumulative distribution of the intensity histogram is then used to remap the image values across the available intensity range.

For a grayscale image, histogram equalization is applied directly to the image intensity. For an RGB image, the image is converted to the Lab color space, histogram equalization is applied to the lightness channel, and the result is converted back to RGB. This enhances contrast while preserving the color information.
Global histogram equalization is useful when the contrast of the whole image can be improved with one intensity mapping. For local contrast enhancement, use imadapthistequal.
If the input image is uint8, the output image is uint8. Other input types are converted back to double after processing.

refsection.title: Examples
